citation_title: IPS CPG: Bipolar Disorder (verify exact title)

rules.explanation_for_clinician: Lithium is a first-line agent for acute mania and for maintenance, with anti-suicidal benefit; it requires baseline and ongoing renal and thyroid monitoring.
missing_investigations: Renal function, thyroid function, calcium; pregnancy test if relevant; ECG if cardiac risk.
monitoring_required: Serum lithium level, renal and thyroid function periodically.

rules.explanation_for_clinician: Second-generation antipsychotics are first-line options for acute mania, alone or combined with a mood stabiliser.
Lamotrigine is an option for the depressive pole of bipolar disorder and for maintenance preventing depressive episodes.
Valproate carries high teratogenic and neurodevelopmental risk; avoid in people of childbearing potential unless other options have failed and an effective pregnancy-prevention plan is in place.
missing_investigations: Documented pregnancy-prevention counselling and pregnancy test before valproate.
